name: GitHub
description: GitHub REST API for agent-driven repository management: repositories, contents and atomic Git writes, branches and commits, issues, pull requests and reviews, Actions runs and artifacts, releases and assets, collaborators, organizations, webhooks, notifications, users, and search.

credential_fields.name: token
credential_fields.label: Personal Access Token
credential_fields.description: GitHub personal access token. Fine-grained tokens are preferred; grant only the repository and organization permissions required by the tools you will use.

title.description: Issue title — pass from get_issue response

tools.name: get_authenticated_user
tools.description: Get the authenticated user's profile including login, name, email, bio, public repos count, followers, and account details

tools.name: get_user
tools.description: Get a user's public profile by their username

tools.name: list_repos
tools.description: List repositories for the authenticated user. Returns repos you own, collaborate on, or are a member of.

visibility.description: Repository visibility filter. Do not combine with type.

affiliation.description: Comma-separated affiliations: owner, collaborator, organization_member. Do not combine with type.

since.description: Only repositories updated after this ISO 8601 timestamp.

before.description: Only repositories updated before this ISO 8601 timestamp.

tools.name: get_repo
tools.description: Get detailed information about a repository including description, stars, forks, language, default branch, and permissions

tools.name: get_archive
tools.description: Download a repository as a gzipped tarball at a given ref (branch, tag, or SHA). Returns binary bytes. GitHub redirects to codeload.github.com; for private repos the redirect Location carries a short-lived signed token so the request succeeds even when the Authorization header is stripped on cross-subdomain redirect. Used by the Code app's import flow.

ref.description: Branch name, tag, or commit SHA. Defaults to the repo's default branch when omitted.

tools.name: list_user_repos
tools.description: List public repositories for a specific user

tools.name: search_repos
tools.description: Search for repositories using GitHub search syntax. Supports qualifiers like language:, stars:>, topic:, etc.

q.description: Search query (supports qualifiers like language:python, stars:>100, topic:machine-learning)

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Search for code across all repositories. Supports qualifiers like repo:, path:, language:, extension:

sort.description: Sort field (only 'indexed' supported). Note: GitHub has flagged sort/order on code search as 'closing down' — prefer omitting both for forward compatibility.

order.description: Sort order. Note: closing down per GitHub — prefer omitting.

tools.name: get_contents
tools.description: Get a repository file or non-root directory listing. For the repository root, use list_root_contents. For files up to 1MB, content is base64 encoded.

path.description: Non-empty path to a file or directory. Use list_root_contents for the root.

ref.description: Branch, tag, or commit SHA (defaults to default branch)

tools.name: create_or_update_file
tools.description: Create or update a file in a repository. Content must be base64 encoded. To update, include the SHA of the file being replaced (from get-contents).

sha.description: SHA of file being replaced (required for updates, get from get-contents)

branch.description: Branch to commit to (defaults to default branch)

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description: Delete a file from a repository. Requires the file's SHA (from get-contents).

path.description: Path to the file to delete

sha.description: SHA of file being deleted (from get-contents)

tools.name: list_branches
tools.description: List branches in a repository

tools.name: create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch. First get the SHA of the source branch via list-branches or get-contents, then create a git ref.

ref.description: Full git ref to create (e.g., refs/heads/my-new-branch)

sha.description: SHA of the commit to branch from

tools.name: list_commits
tools.description: List commits in a repository. Can filter by branch, path, author, and date range.

sha.description: Branch name or commit SHA to start from

path.description: Only commits containing this file path

author.description: GitHub username or email to filter by

since.description: ISO 8601 date — only commits after this date

until.description: ISO 8601 date — only commits before this date

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List issues in a repository. Filter by state, labels, assignee, milestone, and more.

assignee.description: Filter by assignee username (use * for any, none for unassigned)

tools.name: get_issue
tools.description: Get a specific issue by number with full details including body, labels, assignees, and milestone

mock_response.title: Fix login redirect bug
mock_response.body: After logging in, the user is redirected to a blank page.

tools.name: create_issue
tools.description: Create a new issue in a repository with title, body, labels, and assignees

tools.name: update_issue
tools.description: Update an issue — change title, body, state, labels, or assignees

state_reason.description: Reason for the state change. Only valid when transitioning state.

labels.description: Labels to set (replaces all existing labels)

tools.name: list_issue_comments
tools.description: List comments on an issue or pull request

mock_response.body: Thanks for reporting, looking into it.

tools.name: add_issue_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to an issue or pull request

tools.name: search_issues
tools.description: Search issues and pull requests across all repos. Supports qualifiers like repo:, is:pr, is:issue, label:, author:, state:

q.description: Search query (e.g., 'bug label:bug is:open repo:owner/repo', 'is:pr author:username')

tools.name: list_pulls
tools.description: List pull requests in a repository with state, branch, and sort filters

tools.name: get_pull
tools.description: Get a specific pull request with full details including diff stats, mergeable status, and review status

tools.name: create_pull
tools.description: Create a new pull request from a head branch into a base branch

head.description: Branch containing changes (e.g., 'feature-branch' or 'user:feature-branch' for cross-repo)

maintainer_can_modify.description: Allow maintainers of the base repository to push changes to the head branch.

head_repo.description: Head repository name for organization-owned forks when GitHub cannot infer it.

tools.name: merge_pull
tools.description: Merge a pull request. Choose merge method: merge commit, squash, or rebase.

commit_message.description: Extra detail for the merge commit

sha.description: Only merge if the pull request head still matches this SHA.

tools.name: list_pull_files
tools.description: List files changed in a pull request with patch/diff details

tools.name: list_releases
tools.description: List releases in a repository, ordered by creation date

tools.name: create_release
tools.description: Create a new release with a tag, name, and release notes

tag_name.description: Tag name for the release (e.g., v1.0.0)

target_commitish.description: Branch or commit SHA to tag (defaults to default branch)

make_latest.description: Control whether GitHub marks this as the latest release.

discussion_category_name.description: Create a linked discussion in this category when publishing.

tools.name: list_workflows
tools.description: List GitHub Actions workflows in a repository

tools.name: list_workflow_runs
tools.description: List recent runs for a specific workflow

tools.name: trigger_workflow
tools.description: Manually trigger a workflow_dispatch workflow run

ref.description: Branch or tag to run the workflow on

inputs.description: Input parameters for the workflow (key-value pairs)

return_run_details.description: Ask GitHub to return the created workflow run ID and URLs when supported.

tools.name: create_webhook
tools.description: Create a repository webhook to receive event notifications

name.description: Webhook type; GitHub repository webhooks use web.

insecure_ssl.description: Use 0 to verify TLS certificates; 1 disables verification.

events.description: Events to subscribe to (e.g., push, pull_request, issues). Use ['*'] for all.

tools.name: list_webhooks
tools.description: List webhooks configured for a repository

tools.name: fork_repo
tools.description: Fork a repository to the authenticated user's account or a specified organization

organization.description: Organization to fork into (omit to fork to your account)

name.description: Custom name for the fork (defaults to original name)

tools.name: star_repo
tools.description: Star a repository for the authenticated user

tools.name: unstar_repo
tools.description: Remove a star from a repository

tools.name: compare_commits
tools.description: Compare two commits, branches, or tags. Shows files changed and diff stats between base and head.

basehead.description: Comparison in format base...head (e.g., main...feature-branch, v1.0.0...v2.0.0)

tools.name: list_root_contents
tools.description: List files and directories at a repository root, optionally at a branch, tag, or commit SHA.

tools.name: create_repo
tools.description: Create a repository for the authenticated user with initialization and merge-policy options.

tools.name: list_org_repos
tools.description: List repositories belonging to an organization.

tools.name: create_org_repo
tools.description: Create a repository in an organization. The token must have permission to create organization repositories.

tools.name: update_repo
tools.description: Update repository metadata, visibility, feature flags, default branch, archive state, and pull-request merge policies.

tools.name: delete_repo
tools.description: Permanently delete a repository. This is destructive and requires repository administration permission.

tools.name: list_collaborators
tools.description: List repository collaborators and optionally filter by affiliation or permission level.

tools.name: add_collaborator
tools.description: Invite or update a repository collaborator with a specific permission level.

tools.name: remove_collaborator
tools.description: Remove a collaborator's direct access to a repository.

tools.name: get_branch
tools.description: Get one branch, its head commit, protection status, and protection details visible to the token.

tools.name: get_commit
tools.description: Get one repository commit with author, verification, parents, file changes, and diff statistics.

tools.name: get_git_ref
tools.description: Get an exact Git reference such as heads/main or tags/v1.0.0 and its target SHA.

ref.description: Reference without refs/ prefix, for example heads/main.

tools.name: update_git_ref
tools.description: Move an existing branch or tag reference to a commit SHA. Set force only for an intentional non-fast-forward update.

ref.description: Reference without refs/ prefix, for example heads/main.

tools.name: delete_git_ref
tools.description: Delete a branch or tag Git reference. The repository default branch cannot be deleted.

ref.description: Reference without refs/ prefix, for example heads/obsolete.

tools.name: create_git_blob
tools.description: Create a Git blob from UTF-8 or base64 file content. Use its SHA in create_git_tree for atomic multi-file commits.

tools.name: create_git_tree
tools.description: Create a Git tree from blob/tree SHAs or inline file content. Use base_tree to preserve unchanged files.

tools.name: create_git_commit
tools.description: Create a Git commit object from a tree SHA and parent commit SHAs. Move a branch with update_git_ref after creation.

signature.description: ASCII-armored detached PGP signature over the commit payload.

tools.name: create_commit_status
tools.description: Create a pending, success, failure, or error status for a commit SHA and named CI context.

tools.name: update_issue_comment
tools.description: Edit an existing issue or pull-request conversation comment.

tools.name: delete_issue_comment
tools.description: Permanently delete an issue or pull-request conversation comment.

tools.name: list_labels
tools.description: List labels configured in a repository.

tools.name: create_label
tools.description: Create a repository label with a six-character hexadecimal color.

tools.name: add_labels_to_issue
tools.description: Add labels to an issue or pull request without removing its existing labels.

tools.name: lock_issue
tools.description: Lock conversation on an issue or pull request and optionally record a reason.

tools.name: unlock_issue
tools.description: Unlock conversation on an issue or pull request.

tools.name: update_pull
tools.description: Update a pull request title, body, state, base branch, or maintainer-modification permission.

tools.name: list_pull_commits
tools.description: List commits included in a pull request.

tools.name: list_pull_reviews
tools.description: List submitted and pending reviews on a pull request.

tools.name: create_pull_review
tools.description: Create a pull-request review, optionally with inline comments, and submit APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, COMMENT, or leave it pending.

tools.name: list_pull_review_comments
tools.description: List inline code review comments on a pull request.

tools.name: create_pull_review_comment
tools.description: Create an inline review comment on a specific pull-request diff line.

tools.name: request_pull_reviewers
tools.description: Request reviews from users and organization teams.

tools.name: remove_pull_reviewers
tools.description: Remove requested user or team reviewers from a pull request.

tools.name: update_pull_branch
tools.description: Update a pull-request head branch with changes from its base branch.

expected_head_sha.description: Optional concurrency guard; update only if the head still matches this SHA.

tools.name: list_repo_workflow_runs
tools.description: List Actions workflow runs across all workflows in a repository with status, branch, actor, event, date, and SHA filters.

tools.name: get_workflow_run
tools.description: Get one Actions workflow run including status, conclusion, triggering event, head SHA, attempt, and URLs.

tools.name: cancel_workflow_run
tools.description: Cancel an in-progress Actions workflow run.

tools.name: rerun_workflow_run
tools.description: Rerun all jobs in an Actions workflow run, optionally enabling debug logging.

tools.name: list_workflow_run_jobs
tools.description: List jobs and steps for an Actions workflow run, optionally selecting latest or all attempts.

tools.name: download_workflow_run_logs
tools.description: Download a ZIP archive containing logs for every job in an Actions workflow run. Returns binary content.

tools.name: list_repo_artifacts
tools.description: List Actions artifacts across a repository, optionally filtered by exact artifact name.

tools.name: list_workflow_run_artifacts
tools.description: List artifacts produced by one Actions workflow run.

tools.name: get_artifact
tools.description: Get metadata for an Actions artifact including size, expiration, and workflow run.

tools.name: download_artifact
tools.description: Download an Actions artifact as a ZIP archive. Returns binary content and follows GitHub's short-lived redirect.

tools.name: delete_artifact
tools.description: Permanently delete an Actions artifact.

tools.name: get_release
tools.description: Get one release by numeric release ID, including its assets.

tools.name: get_release_by_tag
tools.description: Get a release by its tag name.

tools.name: update_release
tools.description: Update a release's tag, target, title, notes, publication state, prerelease state, and latest-release behavior.

tools.name: delete_release
tools.description: Permanently delete a GitHub release. This does not automatically delete its Git tag.

tools.name: list_release_assets
tools.description: List binary assets attached to a release.

tools.name: upload_release_asset
tools.description: Upload binary content as a release asset through uploads.github.com. Pass a blob reference or binary value plus its filename.

file.description: Binary asset or blob reference. The binary MIME type becomes Content-Type.

tools.name: delete_release_asset
tools.description: Permanently delete a binary release asset.

tools.name: get_webhook
tools.description: Get one repository webhook and its configuration.

tools.name: update_webhook
tools.description: Update a repository webhook URL, signing secret, content type, event subscriptions, or active state.

tools.name: delete_webhook
tools.description: Permanently delete a repository webhook.

tools.name: ping_webhook
tools.description: Send a ping event to a repository webhook to verify delivery.

tools.name: list_webhook_deliveries
tools.description: List recent repository webhook delivery attempts and filter by success or failure.

tools.name: get_webhook_delivery
tools.description: Get request, response, duration, redelivery state, and payload metadata for one webhook delivery.

tools.name: redeliver_webhook_delivery
tools.description: Request redelivery of a previous repository webhook delivery.

tools.name: get_organization
tools.description: Get an organization's public profile and settings visible to the authenticated user.

tools.name: list_organization_members
tools.description: List organization members visible to the token, optionally filtered by role and two-factor state.

tools.name: list_notifications
tools.description: List notifications for the authenticated user, optionally including read notifications or only participating threads.

tools.name: mark_notifications_read
tools.description: Mark notifications as read, optionally only through a given timestamp. Set read=false to mark notifications unread where supported.

tools.name: get_notification_thread
tools.description: Get one notification thread and its subject/repository context.

tools.name: mark_notification_thread_read
tools.description: Mark one notification thread as read.

tools.name: get_rate_limit
tools.description: Get current REST, search, GraphQL, code-scanning, and other GitHub API rate-limit usage for the token.

registration.manual_setup: GitHub repository webhooks require an owner and repository in the registration path. Use github_create_webhook with the subscription callback URL, selected events, and generated secret; use github_delete_webhook to unregister it.

events.name: push
events.description: Any push to a repository branch or tag

events.name: pull_request
events.description: Pull request opened, closed, merged, reopened, or synchronized

events.name: issues
events.description: Issue opened, edited, deleted, closed, reopened, assigned, or labeled

events.name: issue_comment
events.description: Comment created, edited, or deleted on an issue or pull request

events.name: release
events.description: Release published, unpublished, created, edited, or deleted

events.name: workflow_run
events.description: GitHub Actions workflow run requested, completed, or in progress

events.name: pull_request_review
events.description: Pull request review submitted, edited, or dismissed
